explicit_text: I was born in Victoria, British Columbia on July 22, 1926. My father, who was originally from Ukraine, came to Canada as a child and later joined the Naval Reserve, which brought him back to Victoria for training. Victoria was the place where my early life began before we moved to Vancouver in 1935.

explicit_text: My mother was born in Arbroath, Scotland and came to Canada when she was 18, around 1921. She married my father in 1924, and although she insisted we were Scottish at home, her origins in Arbroath were part of our family story. Her Scottish background contrasted with my father's Ukrainian heritage, which was often downplayed in our household.

explicit_text: My father grew up on a wheat farm in Saskatchewan before going overseas with the army during the First World War. explicit_text: My father served in the First World War, but because he had lost the first two joints of two fingers as a child, he couldn't pull a rifle trigger and was assigned to the headquarters branch. He was fortunate to survive and return to Canada, where he later worked as a postmaster and joined the Naval Reserve. His war experience shaped much of his later life.

explicit_text: We moved to Vancouver in 1935 during the Depression, a difficult time because my father was a scab during the long waterfront strike.
